title: Kafka Broker Request Handler Pool Saturation
dataSources: JMX `kafka.server:type=KafkaRequestHandlerPool,name=RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent` ingested as `sourcetype=kafka:serverStats` (Splunk Add-on for JMX KafkaServerStats template)
app: Splunk Add-on for Kafka (`Splunk_TA_kafka`, Splunkbase 2935) + Splunk Add-on for JMX (Splunkbase 2647)
spl:
index=kafka sourcetype="kafka:serverStats"
| where isnotnull(RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent) AND RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent < 0.20
| bin span=5m _time
| stats avg(RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent) as avg_idle by _time, host
| where avg_idle < 0.20
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Low `RequestHandlerAvgIdlePercent` means broker I/O threads are rarely idle, which usually precedes produce/fetch latency spikes and client timeouts during traffic bursts.
value: Surfaces broker CPU/request-thread exhaustion before clients see widespread timeouts, allowing scale-out or partition moves.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to surfaces broker CPU/request-thread exhaustion before clients see widespread timeouts, allowing scale-out or partition moves.
implementation: On a heavy forwarder with `Splunk_TA_kafka` and the JMX add-on, create a KafkaServerStats task per broker (remote JMX enabled). Poll every 60s. Normalize field names to match your JMX template (some builds alias attributes). Alert when the 15-minute rolling average stays below 0.20.
visualization: Line chart (avg_idle by broker), Single value (worst broker), Table (brokers under threshold).

references.title: Configure JMX inputs for the Splunk Add-on for Kafka

knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
